pattern: Summing per-character weights and reducing modulo the alphabet size turns each word into a single letter, and reversing the usual a-to-z direction is just a subtraction from the last letter's code point.
hints: The weight of a word only needs the sum of weights[ord(c) - ord('a')] for every character c in it.
Reduce that sum modulo 26 before mapping it to a letter -- the mapping direction runs backwards from 'z', not forwards from 'a'.
mapped_char = chr(ord('z') - (weight % 26)) turns 0 into 'z' and 25 into 'a'; build the answer by mapping each word in the given order and concatenating.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, three words with varied weights
statement example, uniform weight of 1 for every letter
weight sum is an exact multiple of 26 -- mod 26 must be 0, mapping to 'z'
weight sum mod 26 equals 25 -- must map to 'a', the last case in the reverse mapping
every letter of the alphabet used once at max weight
a maximum-length word to exercise the longest per-word sum

stress.description: random words of random lowercase letters against random weight tables
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 10)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(length)))
    weights = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(26)]
    return [words, weights]
